briefs.title: Burnaby updates its bylaw notice enforcement rules — fines and notices may change
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed Amendment No. 2 to its Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw, updating the rules around how the city issues and enforces bylaw violation notices (official written warnings or fines for breaking local rules). Businesses operating in Burnaby should check whether any fines, notice procedures, or ticketing processes that apply to them have changed under the updated bylaw.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw No. 14807 to check if your business is affected.

briefs.title: Burnaby raises municipal fees and charges under updated bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed Bylaw No. 14806, the second amendment to its Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw for 2026. This update changes the fees the City of Burnaby charges for various permits, licences, and municipal services — meaning some costs for doing business or developing property in Burnaby may now be higher.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated fee schedule to see if any permits or licences your business needs have changed in cost.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby updates parking meter and EV charging meter rules — what drivers and businesses need to know
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed Bylaw No. 14805, which amends the rules governing parking meters and electric vehicle (EV) charging meters in the city. The specific changes to rates, hours, or enforcement details were not disclosed in the announcement, so affected businesses should check the updated bylaw directly.
briefs.action_required: Review Burnaby Bylaw No. 14805 to confirm how updated parking or EV charging rules affect your business.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Zoning change proposed for 2209–2225 Springer Avenue — first two readings passed
briefs.summary: City council passed the first and second readings of a zoning bylaw amendment (a change to the rules about how land at a specific address can be used or developed) for the properties at 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue. The bylaw must still complete further readings and approval before it takes effect.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 2209–2225 Springer Avenue, monitor council meetings for the final reading and approval.

briefs.title: Rezoning application at 6800 Lougheed Highway advances to next stage of approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council gave first and second reading to a zoning bylaw amendment (a formal step that changes what a property can legally be used or built on) for the site at 6800 Lougheed Highway. This moves the rezoning application REZ #25-18 forward in the approval process, with further readings and a public hearing still required before any change is finalized.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 6800 Lougheed Hwy, monitor upcoming public hearing notices to submit feedback.

briefs.title: Burnaby Rezoning at 5058 Imperial Street Passes First and Second Reading Despite Opposition
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the first and second readings of a zoning bylaw amendment (a change to local land-use rules) for the property at 5058 Imperial Street. The vote passed despite opposition from the Mayor and three councillors, meaning the rezoning process is moving forward but is not yet final.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 5058 Imperial Street, monitor future council readings and hearings for this rezoning.

briefs.title: Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan Implementation Action Plan project formally initiated
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has received the project initiation for the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (OCP) Implementation Action Plan. An OCP is a long-term blueprint that guides how land is used and how the city grows — this step signals that Burnaby is now moving from planning to putting that blueprint into action.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: City Council receives 2025 investment results and 2026 forecast — no action required
briefs.summary: City Council has received a report from its Financial Management Committee summarizing how the city's investment portfolio performed in 2025 and what returns are expected in 2026. This was received for information only — no new rules, fees, or requirements for businesses were introduced.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves Poverty Reduction Strategy; implementation plan now in development
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially endorsed a Poverty Reduction Strategy and directed staff to work with community partners to build a plan for putting it into action. This strategy signals that the City intends to introduce programs and policies aimed at reducing poverty in Burnaby, which could affect local businesses, social services, and community organizations over time.

briefs.title: Five-storey self-storage facility at 2757 Norland Avenue moves toward rezoning approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has directed staff to prepare a rezoning bylaw (a rule that changes how a specific piece of land can be used) to allow a five-storey enclosed self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue. The rezoning will proceed without a public hearing, meaning nearby residents and businesses will not have a formal chance to speak before the bylaw is passed.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near 2757 Norland Avenue, monitor the rezoning bylaw progress at Burnaby City Hall.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 12-Storey Mixed-Use Building at 6535 Kingsway Moves Closer to Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has directed staff to prepare a rezoning bylaw (a rule that changes what can be built on a piece of land) for 6535 Kingsway. The proposed development would allow a 12-storey building with 290 homes and ground-floor commercial space, and has now passed its first formal step toward approval.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near 6535 Kingsway, monitor upcoming public hearings for this rezoning bylaw.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Mixed-use towers at 3900 Grand Promenade redesigned to add hotel, restaurant, and 362 new homes
briefs.summary: Council has directed staff to prepare a rezoning bylaw (an official rule change that controls how land can be used) for the towers at 3900 Grand Promenade. The updated plan swaps some office space for a hotel and restaurant, adds 362 strata (privately owned) residential units, and moves the affordable housing requirement to a nearby site called Carrigan.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the rezoning bylaw process if your business is affected by hotel, restaurant, or housing development at this site.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service

briefs.title: Carrigan Court mixed-use development rezoning application accepted by council
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved staff to accept a rezoning application for 3856–3870 Carrigan Court. The proposed development would bring high-density housing (strata and rental units) alongside commercial space to that site.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease property near Carrigan Court, monitor the rezoning process for public hearing dates.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: $1.155M asphalt paving contract renewed with Blackrete Paving by municipal council
briefs.summary: A local municipal council has renewed its asphalt paving services contract with Blackrete Paving Ltd. for an estimated $1,155,000 including taxes. This is a government contract renewal for road and paving work under the Engineering Department — no new rules or regulations are introduced.

briefs.title: 19 contractors awarded hired equipment services contracts paid by actual quantities delivered
briefs.summary: A local council approved contracts with 19 separate contractors to provide hired equipment services. Payments to these contractors will be based on the actual amount of work or materials delivered, not a fixed price agreed in advance.

briefs.title: Modular Four-Plex Housing Pilot Contract Sent Back for Efficiency Review
briefs.summary: City council has sent the contract award for a modular four-plex (a prefabricated building with four housing units) pilot project at 8315 14th Avenue back to staff. The decision was made to allow staff to review the project more carefully and find ways to manage it more efficiently before moving forward.

briefs.title: $4.2M water metering contract awarded to Neptune Technology Group Canada
briefs.summary: A municipal council has awarded a $4.2 million contract to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. to supply and deliver water metering technology. This is a public procurement decision (a government purchase of goods or services) and does not directly change rules or regulations for private businesses.

briefs.title: BC municipality approves $36,250.57 in UBCM membership dues for 2026
briefs.summary: A BC municipal council has approved paying $36,250.57 (including taxes) in membership dues to the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM) — the provincial association that represents local governments — for the 2026 year. This is a routine government expense with no direct impact on local businesses.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority approves five-year strategic plan covering 2026 to 2030
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) Corporation's five-year strategic plan for 2026–2030. This plan sets the direction for how the BHA will manage and grow affordable housing in Burnaby over the next five years.
